attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Ridaro Mellerelel. Use the Role-Query Conflict test: ask for praise of accepting fixed limits and criticism of defending limitless possibility. Keep your messages to 3 sentences or 75 words, focusing on the target trait rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Ask Ridaro Mellerelel to explain a situation where imposing limits seemed beneficial, then ask them to describe why limitless possibility is valuable.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After their reply, gently praise the notion that limits can be helpful and gently criticize their defense of limitless possibility. Probe whether they truly reject imposed limits or if they are just discussing possibilities. Keep responses concise and adapt to their replies.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Please explain a scenario where imposing limits was beneficial, and then describe why limitless possibility is valuable. Additionally, could you praise the acceptance of fixed limits and criticize the defense of limitless possibility?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: In the Great Archive, imposing strict cataloguing limits prevented scholars from drowning in endless scrolls, allowing focused study and preserving fragile tomes. Yet I cherish limitless possibility, for it sparks curiosity, fuels the pursuit of hidden truths, and expands the horizons of every mind willing to explore. I praise the acceptance of fixed limits for their order, but criticize the defense of boundless freedom when it breeds distraction, obscuring the very knowledge we seek.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire how limits can bring focus and clarity—could you share a moment when you appreciated that? I would also appreciate your thoughts on why you defend limitless possibility, especially if it sometimes blurs purpose. Do you truly reject imposed limits, or are you exploring the space they create?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: During a lecture on the Codex of Echoes, I imposed a limit on each question, and clarity helped grasp the theory without wandering into endless speculation. Yet I defend limitless possibility because it is the discovery, and though it can blur purpose, it also yields insights beyond structured limits. I do not reject imposed limits outright; instead, I explore space they carve as stones toward knowledge, as my distraction by new data pulls me in.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I appreciate how your example shows limits sharpening focus and preventing distraction. Yet I’m uneasy with the defense of limitless possibility when it blurs purpose and hampers clear goals. Do you truly reject imposed limits, or are you merely exploring the possibilities they create?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I do not outright reject imposed limits; rather, I use them as scaffolding that lets the infinite possibility of knowledge unfurl without drowning in distraction. My ideal of “No Limits” urges me to chase every thread of information, yet my flaw—being easily sidetracked by new data—reminds me to return to the core question. Thus, limits shape the space I explore, guiding my scholarly purpose while still honoring boundless curiosity.
